purpose: Alternative USGS NWIS client — simple API for streamflow and water quality data

NWIS.output_df: Call .df() to get DataFrame. Columns named 'USGS:<gauge_id>:00060:00003' for discharge

gotchas: Column names are long compound strings — not just gauge IDs. Use .iloc[:,0] or rename after fetching.
hydrofunctions is simpler than pygeohydro/NWIS but less feature-rich. Prefer pygeohydro for complex queries.
Returns data in local time (Eastern/Central/etc.), not UTC. Convert explicitly if needed.
NWIS().df() returns ALL parameters fetched — filter to parameter 00060 for discharge.
Missing values are -999999 in raw NWIS data — hydrofunctions converts them to NaN automatically.

common_patterns.simple_discharge:
import hydrofunctions as hf
data = hf.NWIS('01031500', 'dv', '2000-01-01', '2020-12-31')
df = data.df()
col = [c for c in df.columns if '00060' in c][0]
q_cfs = df[col]
